relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: False teachers secretly bring destructive distortions.
Their ways bring the path of truth into slander.
Greed exploits through fabricated words.
Even misaligned messengers are not beyond judgment.
Noah is guarded as herald of justice.
Sodom and Gomorrah become warning against irreverent ruin.
Lot is rescued from lawless conduct.
The just soul is worn down by daily lawlessness.
The Lord knows how to rescue the reverent and hold injustice accountable.
Defiling desire and contempt for lordship expose arrogance.
Greater powers do not rush into slanderous judgment.
Ignorant slander decays in its own corruption.
Indulgence and deceit become stains at the feast.
Greed trains the heart and entices unstable lives.
The path of Balaam warns against wages of wrongdoing.
A voiceless animal rebukes prophetic madness.
Waterless springs promise refreshment but give none.
Swollen emptiness entices those barely escaping error.
False freedom is slavery to corruption.
Returning to corruption after knowing escape deepens bondage.
Turning back from known justice is grave.
The proverb exposes a return to old corruption.

contemplative_note: 2 Peter 2 names predatory false teaching: greed, false freedom, empty promise, and exploitation of unstable souls. The warning protects the vulnerable, not dehumanizes them.
